data.title: WBA DEADLINE MONDAY: ROSEN, SKILLED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important March 31 Deadline in Securities Class Action - WBA
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --

WHY: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, reminds purchasers of common stock of Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc. (NASDAQ: WBA) between April 2, 2020 and January 16, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”), of the important March 31, 2025 lead plaintiff deadline.

SO WHAT: If you purchased Walgreens common stock during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

WHAT TO DO NEXT: To join the Walgreens class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=27235 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than March 31, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

WHY ROSEN LAW: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Many of these firms do not actually litigate securities class actions, but are merely middlemen that refer clients or partner with law firms that actually litigate the cases. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company at the time. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs’ Bar. Many of the firm’s attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

DETAILS OF THE CASE: According to the lawsuit, defendants, throughout the Class Period, failed to disclose to investors that: (1) contrary to Walgreens’ purported commitment to improved regulatory compliance, Walgreens continued to engage in widespread violations of federal law governing the dispensation of prescription medication and reimbursement of the same; (2) the foregoing conduct, when revealed, would subject Walgreens to a heightened risk of further regulatory scrutiny, civil liability, and reputational harm; (3) Walgreens’ revenues from the sale of prescription medications were unsustainable to the extent that they derived from unlawful conduct; and (4) as a result, Walgreens’ public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.   When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.
To join the Walgreens class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=27235 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. You may also remain an absent class member and do nothing at this point. An investor’s ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm, on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm/.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

-------------------------------

Contact Information:

        Laurence Rosen, Esq.
        Phillip Kim, Esq.
        The Rosen Law Firm, P.A.
        275 Madison Avenue, 40th Floor
        New York, NY 10016
        Tel: (212) 686-1060
        Toll Free: (866) 767-3653
        Fax: (212) 202-3827
        case@rosenlegal.com
        www.rosenlegal.com

data.title: President Trump Pardons Three BitMex Co-Founders, CNBC Reports
data.description:
--President Trump has pardoned three co-founders of the cryptocurrency exchange BitMex, CNBC reported Friday it had learned.

--The co-founders, Arthur Hayes, Benjamin Delo and Samuel Reed, previously pled guilty to charges related to money laundering, according to the report.

--Trump issued the pardons Thursday, the report said. 




  Full article at https://www.cnbc.com/2025/03/28/trump-pardon-bitmex-crypto-exchange-money-laundering.html 





  Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 28, 2025 15:22 ET (19:22 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: After 10 years of Private Listings, here is what is next at MRED
data.description:
LISLE, Ill., March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Midwest Real Estate Data (MRED), one of the nation’s largest multiple listing services (MLS), has been on the forefront of changes in listing marketing options with its Private Listings since 2016. The National Association of REALTORS® (NAR) recently announced its changes to the Clear Cooperation Policy for its affiliated MLSs. MRED is pleased to see the changes, as they are aligned with MRED’s current solution that has existed for the last decade. 

Today, MRED shares an update to its white paper, “Private, Not a Secret,” (originally published in 2019) with statistics highlighting usage of Private Listings and a look into what is coming soon. 

“MRED exists to provide top-performing real estate professionals the tools they need to serve their clients,” said MRED President and CEO Rebecca Jensen. “Real estate transactions are very nuanced, and one size does not fit all. This is why MRED’s solution equips professionals with the information they need, where they need it. Our latest changes in listing controls are an extension of this long-held philosophy.”   

The updated white paper shows how MRED’s solution—first adopted in 2016—has helped agents better serve clients, reduce confusion, and reinforce cooperation. It also offers insight into MRED’s unique solution, as well as highlighting what is coming next for brokers in listing marketing. 

Read the full white paper here: https://bit.ly/4j6cTEJ 

About MRED 
Midwest Real Estate Data (MRED) is the multiple listing service (MLS) for real estate professionals across northern Illinois, southern Wisconsin, and northwest Indiana. MRED connects real estate professionals with the tools, data, and support they need to serve clients and grow their businesses. 


Jeremy Sharp
Midwest Real Estate Data LLC
630-799-1402
jeremy.sharp@mredllc.com

data.title: What will DOGE mean for your Social Security checks? Elon Musk's Fox interview offers more questions than answers.
data.description:
By Brett Arends

Musk and DOGE staffers appeared on Fox News Thursday in an effort to quell rising doubts and concerns about the Trump administration's ongoing budget-cutting campaign

When it comes to fraud, Social Security and the latest big claims being made by Elon Musk, the key question isn't how many times a day criminals try to steal money.

It's how many times they succeed - and how much money they walk away with.

Musk and his DOGE team raised more questions than they answered Thursday night in a long and detailed interview with Bret Baier on Fox News, in which the Tesla (TSLA) boss and the Trump administration as a whole sought to quell rising doubts and concerns about their ongoing budget-cutting campaign.

Engineer Aram Moghaddassi, one of seven staffers from the entity known as the Department of Government Efficiency who appeared alongside Musk, said that Social Security Administration call centers are swamped with phone calls from fraudsters trying to redirect individuals' benefits to their own bank accounts.

"At Social Security, one of the first things we learned is that they get phone calls every day [from] people trying to change direct-deposit information, [and] we learned 40% of the phone calls that they get are from fraudsters," Moghaddassi said.

"They steal people's Social Security is what happens," Musk added. "They call in, they claim to be a retiree, then they convince the Social Security person on the phone to change where the money's flowing. It actually goes to some fraudster. This is happening all day, every day."

The claim that 40% of all calls to Social Security call centers are made by fraudsters is a remarkable one, assuming it is correct. Fox's Baier repeated the figure with astonishment.

But this in itself would have no effect on Social Security's budget, let alone on Musk's repeated claims that he can slash $1 trillion, or 15%, from the entire federal budget just by cutting waste, fraud and abuse.

We might as well try to estimate how much money someone lost to fraud in a given year by the counting the number of emails they received from Nigerian princes.

The idea that the federal government is being raided by professional gangs of fraudsters - in other words, by organized crime - is intriguing. It's an argument that was made nearly two months ago in testimony to Congress by Haywood Talcove, the chief executive of LexisNexis Special Services, a management consultancy that focuses on government. After Talcove's appearance, I called him up and we had a long conversation about federal fraud. Talcove has a sophisticated analysis of fraud and the federal government that is at odds with some of the simplistic and cartoonish ideas being bandied about by some parts of Team MAGA.

Talcove argues that the real issue isn't individual deadbeats stealing from Uncle Sam, but organized crime - domestic and international gangs using increasingly sophisticated techniques to rip off the U.S. Treasury via programs such as Social Security, food stamps and so on. Supposedly their job is made easier by the federal government's propensity for bureaucracy and its use of outmoded IT systems.

It's a plausible argument. But, as with Musk's claims, it's pretty much impossible to prove or disprove from the outside. We should all be skeptical of arguments that rest on ideology, partisanship or tribalism.

The proof of the DOGE pudding will be in the eating. To put it another way, we will not really know how much cash Elon Musk can save taxpayers until he comes back at the end of this process and can point to it.

Meanwhile, this wasn't the only issue related to Social Security that came up during the Musk interview and that demands more information.

It was one thing for the world's richest man to promise that his budget-slashing DOGE committee wouldn't decrease the amount of your Social Security benefits.

But what exactly did he mean when he said he was going to increase them?

"Legitimate people as a result of the work of DOGE will receive more Social Security, not less," Musk said. "I want to emphasize that. As a result of the work of DOGE, legitimate recipients of Social Security will receive more money, not less."

We have to assume that DOGE is not planning any unilateral increase in anyone's benefits - something that could only happen by an act of Congress, and that is directly contrary to the Trump administration's budget-cutting agenda. You do not achieve Musk's goal of cutting "$1 trillion" from federal spending, or about 15% of the total, by randomly and illegally deciding to spend more on the biggest single government program.

So what does the puzzling statement mean? Neither Musk nor the White House could immediately be reached to explain Musk's claim in detail. Stay tuned.

-Brett Arends

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  03-28-25 1506ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Allan Boomer of Momentum Advisors Launches Scholarship to Support First-Generation College Students Across the U.S.
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- The Allan Boomer Scholarship for First Generation College Students is now open for applications, offering a $1,000 award to undergraduate students who are the first in their families to attend college. Created to recognize and support students forging their own path in higher education, this scholarship underscores the importance of academic ambition, perseverance, and long-term professional growth.

Founded by Allan Boomer, a seasoned financial expert and the Chief Investment Officer of Momentum Advisors, the scholarship is a reflection of his commitment to education and community development. With a distinguished career spanning investment management and financial planning, Allan Boomer understands the transformative power of education and seeks to support students facing unique challenges as first-generation scholars.

Scholarship Details and Application Process

The Allan Boomer Scholarship for First Generation College Students is open to undergraduate students enrolled in accredited institutions across the United States. To apply, students must submit a 700-1,000 word essay answering the following prompt:

“What challenges have you faced as a first-generation college student, and how have they shaped your academic and career aspirations?”

Applications should showcase originality, depth of thought, and a clear vision for the future. Submissions must be sent to apply@allanboomerscholarship.com no later than December 15, 2025. The recipient will be announced on January 15, 2026.

Further details can be found on the official website:
https://allanboomerscholarship.com/

A Commitment to Higher Education

Having built a career advising individuals and institutions on financial growth, Allan Boomer is dedicated to creating opportunities for those determined to succeed despite obstacles. The scholarship serves as an extension of this commitment, helping first-generation students gain the financial support they need to achieve their academic goals.

“There is something truly remarkable about being the first in a family to attend college,” said Allan Boomer. “It requires resilience, determination, and an unwavering belief in the value of education. This scholarship is about recognizing and supporting those who take that courageous step forward.”

The scholarship is open to students in all fields of study, reinforcing the belief that education in any discipline can be a catalyst for positive change.

About Allan Boomer

Allan Boomer is the founder and Chief Investment Officer of Momentum Advisors, a New York City-based investment management firm that oversees nearly $400 million in assets. His expertise in wealth management stems from years of experience at Goldman Sachs and Merrill Lynch, where he developed strategic financial solutions for individuals and businesses. Beyond finance, Allan Boomer is passionate about mentoring, community service, and expanding access to financial education.

The Allan Boomer Scholarship for First Generation College Students is a testament to his mission of supporting students who are navigating the challenges of higher education for the first time in their families.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their essays via email at apply@allanboomerscholarship.com. The deadline to apply is December 15, 2025, and the winner will be announced on January 15, 2026.

For full details on eligibility and submission guidelines, visit:
https://allanboomerscholarship.com/allan-boomer-scholarship/

Contact Info:

Spokesperson: Allan Boomer

Organization: Allan Boomer Scholarship

Website: https://allanboomerscholarship.com   

Email: apply@allanboomerscholarship.com

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/ae6ecfd3-b127-496b-a32b-0ca8d4e1a3a1


Allan Boomer

Allan Boomer

data.title: Protect your assets - and your family - with a better estate plan
data.description:
By Bob Peterson

Avoid financial disaster and keep your wealth away from bad actors

Life is unpredictable. Even the most carefully laid plans can go awry. But while life is full of the unexpected, estate plans can help you control the impact that the unexpected can have on your family's wealth.

When certain situations arise in your own life, or in the lives of your family members and other beneficiaries, you and your financial adviser can review your estate plan to pre-emptively protect your loved ones from making mistakes leading to financial disaster-and prevent the wealth you've worked so hard to accumulate from going to bad actors.

There are several life events-both expected and unexpected-that should prompt you to review your estate planning documents.

Getting married

In my experience, the majority of single people don't have an estate plan in place, unless they are part of high-net-worth or ultra-high-net-worth families and have inherited wealth. But when you get married, everything changes. You and your spouse should at least have a last will and testament, to ensure they receive any of your assets in the event of an untimely passing (and vice versa).

Too many people tend to be put off by what they believe will be an exorbitant fee to create a last will and testament, or they feel they don't have enough assets to require a will or a more complex estate plan. But in my experience as an adviser, spending $3,000 upfront to put financial protections and beneficiaries, and powers of attorney, in writing is a lot cheaper than $20,000 in attorneys' fees in legal wrangling.

Beyond your will, you should also establish a power of attorney (POA) for healthcare and a power of attorney for property. The POA for healthcare will grant your spouse the ability to make important medical decisions upon your physical or mental incapacity. The POA for property is just as important, granting your spouse the ability to access and make financial decisions if you are incapacitated.

Read: Prevent drama with your will by adding this to your estate plan

Having a child

The next major life cycle event is having a child. When you drafted your original last will and testament, you were only concerned about you and your spouse. The addition of a new family member brings a new array of concerns and issues that must be addressed.

Although your powers of attorney will not require an update, your last will and testament will need some changes:

Guardianship

If one of you dies, the other spouse will of course raise your child, but what happens if you are both deceased before the child reaches the age of majority (18 in some states, but higher or lower in others)? Your will should not only name a primary guardian in case you are both deceased, but several backups as well. This should be revisited every few years to make sure you are still comfortable with your choices.

Protection of assets

If you die without a last will and testament, the assets will be held for your children in a guardianship account until they reach the age of majority. However, assuming you have accumulated assets and life insurance, do you really want an 18-year-old to have unfettered access to a large amount of funds?

I frequently ask clients, "What would you have done with $1 million when you were 18, or 21?"

I know I would have made dramatically different choices with that amount of money when I was 18 and 21 than I would now. And the clients I pose that question to usually answer the same, after remembering what they were like at those ages.

Instead of granting your children access to all of your wealth when they reach the legal age of maturity, it is better to revise your will to create a testamentary trust (one established upon your death) or, depending on your net worth, a separate revocable living trust to protect the assets. These types of trusts will give the child access to income and principal for health, education, maintenance, and support, but will prevent large principal distributions until they are older.

Struggling with addiction

One of the biggest advantages of trusts is that they can prevent people who are due to inherit a lot of money at a young age from making impulsive or immature decisions that could deplete their wealth. But apart from impulsivity and immaturity, which frequently affect the judgment of young people, there are other influences that parents need to take into consideration when thinking about how their children would handle the inheritance of significant wealth at too young of an age.

If your child develops a problem with drugs or alcohol as a teenager, trusts are probably the last thing on your mind as a parent. But this is something that must be addressed, because if your child suffers a relapse after coming of age and inheriting a lot of money as the trustee of their own trust, they could go through their money very quickly.

In a situation like this, a parent of a child suffering from addiction can make arrangements for an independent trustee to oversee their child's trust, in order to ensure the money will not be spent on drugs and alcohol.

Adult children in bad marriages

When our children get married, we hope it will last forever. But if you have a son or daughter who is in a marriage that has become rocky or toxic, then you will look to take measures to prevent your child's eventual ex-spouse from inheriting wealth, including family homes or heirlooms.

Before I worked with him, one of my clients sold a secondary home at an appraised value to his daughter, who was in a sour marriage at the time. Instead of passing the home to his daughter through a trust, the home went directly to her, so during his daughter's divorce, her soon-to-be-ex-husband got half of her assets, including the property she received from her parents. The home nearly quadrupled in value over the course of four years, from $750,000 to $2.5 million. So the ex-husband walked away with a lot more money than my client or his daughter wanted him to-and all because the home was transferred directly to her while she was still married.

Aside from life events, legislative developments can also require individuals and families to review their estate plans. For example, the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017 increased the lifetime gift and estate tax exemption to $13.99 million for individuals and $27.98 million for married couples for 2025. These increases are set to expire on Jan. 1, 2026. However, with Republicans in control of the presidency and both houses of Congress, there is a high likelihood the current tax code may be extended. Furthermore, these exemptions vary on a state level, so high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth individuals and families should find out how these changes may affect their planned lifetime giving.

Life has many twists and turns, but estate plans can at least provide some definitive control over our financial lives-and those of our spouses and children. Amid unexpected or undesirable circumstances, a trusted financial adviser can work with you to revisit your estate plans to make sure you financially protect yourselves and your loved ones.

Bob Peterson, J.D., is senior wealth advisor at Crescent Grove Advisors, an employee-owned Registered Investment Advisor based in Milwaukee.

-Bob Peterson

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  03-28-25 1449ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: buildOn's Better Together Concert Returns to San Francisco with Blues Phenom Grace Bowers & The Hodge Podge
data.description:
buildOn's Better Together Concert Returns to San Francisco with Blues Phenom Grace Bowers & The Hodge Podge

buildOn's Better Together Concert Returns to San Francisco with Blues Phenom Grace Bowers & The Hodge Podge

PR Newswire

SAN FRANCISCO, March 28, 2025

Annual benefit concert celebrates Oakland youth while raising funds for transformational Service Learning Program

SAN FRANCISCO, March 28, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Get ready to be electrified! buildOn is thrilled to announce that blues sensation Grace Bowers & The Hodge Podge will headline the 2025 Better Together Concert on Thursday, May 8, 2025, at The Chapel at 777 Valencia Street in San Francisco's vibrant Mission District. This unforgettable night of music and community will celebrate local students while raising critical funds for buildOn's Service Learning Program in Oakland. Doors will open at 6:30.

18-year-old guitar prodigy Grace Bowers, hailed by Rolling Stone as "Nashville's new guitar hero," will bring her fiery talent to the stage following the breakout success of her debut album Wine On Venus. Produced by John Osborne of Brothers Osborne, the album has been praised as "an infectious, joyous party" by Forbes, cementing Bowers' status as one of music's most exciting rising stars.

"This concert embodies what makes Oakland special — incredible artistry meeting deep community commitment," said Sterling Spence, buildOn's Executive Director in Oakland. "When you hear Grace's guitar light up the room, you'll feel the same energy our students bring to serving their neighborhoods. Every ticket sold helps us support our young leaders as they transform Oakland through action."

All proceeds from the Better Together Concert will directly support buildOn's work in Oakland, where students contribute thousands of service hours annually to address local challenges while developing leadership skills. The organization's Service Learning Program currently engages nearly 700 Oakland students in transformative volunteer projects every week. Tickets are available now at buildon.org/bettertogether.

To learn more about buildOn's work in Oakland, visit buildon.org/oakland.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/buildons-better-together-concert-returns-to-san-francisco-with-blues-phenom-grace-bowers--the-hodge-podge-302414575.html

SOURCE buildOn, Inc.

data.title: TRSG Announcement: If You Have Suffered Losses in Tungray Technologies Inc (NASDAQ: TRSG), You Are Encouraged to Contact The Rosen Law Firm About Your Rights
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --

WHY: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, continues to investigate potential securities claims on behalf of shareholders of Tungray Technologies Inc (NASDAQ: TRSG) resulting from allegations that Tungray Technologies may have issued materially misleading business information to the investing public.

SO WHAT: If you purchased Tungray Technologies securities you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement. The Rosen Law Firm is preparing a class action seeking recovery of investor losses.

WHAT TO DO NEXT: To join the prospective class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=32968 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action.

WHAT IS THIS ABOUT: On December 31, 2024, Tungray Technologies filed a current report with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Form 6-K. The current report stated that on “December 30, 2024, the Board of Directors of Tungray Technologies Inc (the “Company”), upon recommendation of the Audit Committee and following discussions with management, determined that the Company’s financial statements for the years ended December 31, 2023, 2022 and 2021 included in the Company’s Annual Report on Form 20-F for the year ended December 31, 2023, filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on April 26, 2024 (the “20-F”), should no longer be relied upon. Similarly, related reports, press releases, earnings releases, and investor communications describing the Company’s financial statements for these periods should no longer be relied upon.”

WHY ROSEN LAW: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Many of these firms do not actually litigate securities class actions. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company at the time. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs’ Bar. Many of the firm’s attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.
